rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical diselenylation/cyclization of o-divinylbenzenes with diaryl diselenides to give 1,3-diselenyl-1,3-dihydroisobenzofurans
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diphenyl diselenide to a radical cation that fragments into PhSe• and PhSe+; subsequent anodic oxidations of benzylic radicals to cations; cathodic reduction of protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: benzylic C-radical II from PhSe• addition to one vinyl group
benzylic carbocation III (after anodic oxidation of II)
benzylic alcohol intermediate IV (after H2O attack and deprotonation)
C-radical V from PhSe• addition to remaining vinyl
benzylic cation VI (after anodic oxidation of V)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh to a radical cation that fragments into PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe• adds to one alkene of the o-divinylbenzene to give benzylic radical II, which is anodically oxidized to cation III and trapped by H2O to give alcohol IV; PhSe• then adds to the second alkene giving radical V, which is again anodically oxidized to cation VI and intramolecularly trapped by the pendant hydroxyl to close the dihydroisobenzofuran; protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: H2 18O labeling
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporated into the dihydroisobenzofuran oxygen, indicating the ring oxygen originates from water

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: PhSeSePh (2a) is oxidized at a significantly lower potential than substrate 1a, supporting initial anodic oxidation of the diselenide

probe_reagents.name: N2 atmosphere control
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeds smoothly under N2, indicating O2 is not required as oxidant

notes: Authors did not explicitly run radical-trap (TEMPO/BHT) experiments in the shown text; mechanism invokes sequential radical addition / anodic oxidation / nucleophilic trapping cycles (radical-polar crossover). X-ray structure of 3aa (CCDC 2143795) confirms product connectivity and diastereochemistry.
